features.name: Smooth Multi-Page Product Tour
features.description: Replace react-joyride with driver.js for a smooth, multi-page onboarding tour that navigates users through each page with no overlay flashing. Each sidebar item is highlighted, clicking Next navigates to the page and shows page-level details.

stories.title: Install driver.js and remove react-joyride
acceptance: driver.js is installed as a dependency
react-joyride and react-floater are uninstalled
No remaining imports of react-joyride in the codebase
Build succeeds with no errors

stories.title: Create driver.js CSS theme matching glassmorphic design system
acceptance: driver.js CSS custom properties overridden in global CSS (src/index.css or similar)
Popover background: rgba(17, 24, 39, 0.95) with backdrop-blur(12px)
Border: 1px solid rgba(124, 58, 237, 0.35) (violet-600/35 glow)
Box-shadow matches existing: 0 25px 50px -12px rgba(0,0,0,0.6), 0 0 0 1px rgba(124,58,237,0.15)
Border-radius: 1rem (16px) on popover
Title: text-white, 1rem, font-weight 700
Body: text-gray-300, 0.875rem, line-height 1.5
Next button: bg-violet-600, rounded-lg, text-sm font-medium, white text
Back button: text-gray-400
Skip button: text-gray-500
Progress dots match SetupWizard stepper pattern: active=wide pill, inactive=small dot
Overlay color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.55)
Spotlight border-radius: 0.5rem
Smooth spotlight transition: CSS transition on all position/size properties ~350ms ease-in-out
Dark mode and light mode both look correct

stories.title: Add data-tour targets on each page for page-level highlights
acceptance: Dashboard page: data-tour='dashboard-overview' on the main metrics/overview section
Meetings page: data-tour='meetings-list' on the UnifiedMeetingsList container
Intelligence page: data-tour='intelligence-dashboard' on the dashboard tab content area
Integrations page: data-tour='integrations-grid' on the integration cards grid
Settings page: data-tour='settings-hub' on the settings cards container
Credits widget (top bar): already has data-tour='credits' — verify it works
No visual changes to any page — just data attributes added

stories.title: Build ProductTour component with driver.js multi-page navigation
acceptance: ProductTour.tsx rewritten to use driver.js instead of react-joyride
Tour has ~10 steps in this flow: sidebar highlight -> page detail for each section
Step 1: Highlight sidebar 'Dashboard' — tooltip: 'Your Command Centre' — placement right
Step 2: Navigate to /dashboard, highlight dashboard-overview — tooltip: page details (metrics, targets, activity)
Step 3: Highlight sidebar 'Meetings' — tooltip: 'Meeting Intelligence'
Step 4: Navigate to /meetings, highlight meetings-list — tooltip: page details (all meetings, AI summaries, sources)
Step 5: Highlight sidebar 'Intelligence' — tooltip: 'Analytics & Insights'
Step 6: Navigate to /meeting-analytics, highlight intelligence-dashboard — tooltip: page details (talk ratios, sentiment, coaching)
Step 7: Highlight sidebar 'Integrations' — tooltip: 'Connect Your Tools'
Step 8: Navigate to /integrations, highlight integrations-grid — tooltip: page details (CRM, calendar, email connections)
Step 9: Highlight sidebar 'Settings' — tooltip: 'Your Settings'
Step 10: Highlight credits widget — tooltip: 'AI Credits' — final step with 'Let's Go' button
onNextClick handler: navigates to correct route before advancing step, with 150ms delay for DOM mount
onPrevClick handler: navigates back to correct route
Tour dismissal persists to localStorage (sixty_tour_completed_{userId})
Tour only shows within 1 hour of onboarding completion (existing logic preserved)
Tour starts after 4000ms delay (existing WelcomeSplash wait preserved)
On tour complete: calls useSetupWizardStore.getState().openWizard() (existing behavior)
On skip: does NOT open wizard (existing behavior)
Spotlight moves smoothly between sidebar items — no flashing
Spotlight moves smoothly from sidebar to page content — no flashing
Overlay stays constant throughout — never unmounts

stories.title: Polish tour UX: edge cases, keyboard nav, and final design pass
acceptance: ESC key dismisses the tour (driver.js default — verify it works)
Clicking overlay dismisses the tour (verify or disable based on preference)
Window resize recalculates spotlight position correctly
Tour handles edge case: target element not found (skip step gracefully)
Tour progress indicator shows current step / total (e.g., '3 of 10')
Last step button text is 'Let's Go' instead of 'Next'
First step has no 'Back' button
All tooltip content is concise and action-oriented
Tour works in both expanded and collapsed sidebar states
Navigating away from the page during tour gracefully cleans up
No console errors during tour flow
Verify smooth spotlight animation on each transition (no flash, smooth slide)
Design review: tooltips match glassmorphic violet theme from TOUR-002

parallelGroups.reason: CSS theme and data-tour attributes are independent — can run in parallel after TOUR-001

designNotes.libraryChoice: driver.js (~5kb) replaces react-joyride (broken overlay remount architecture)
designNotes.whyNotJoyride: react-joyride unmounts/remounts the Portal DOM node on every step change. CSS transitions have nothing to animate against because the element is destroyed and recreated. This is a known, unresolved issue (GitHub #872, #918, #973).
designNotes.whyDriverJs: Single persistent DOM element for overlay — spotlight moves via CSS transitions, no DOM remount. Built-in onNextClick hook for page navigation. 5kb gzipped. Active maintenance.
designNotes.whyNotCustom: framer-motion custom build (~200 lines + floating-ui) is overkill for a 10-step tour. Revisit if tour becomes a core product surface with branching logic.
